methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Fourth Power, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Asegun Henry, is evaluated against a peer universe of 8 private companies and 1 public comparable in the same sector and stage band. The private cohort includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., with valuations ranging from $110M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the company's last post-money valuation of $150M, implying $300M. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company climate and energy cohort yields a median valuation of $305M, with a range spanning $110M to $5.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier energy.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $255M to $351M, with a median of $302M. This suggests the current $150M post-money valuation reflects a 2.0x to 2.3x step-up to next-stage funding. Key uncertainties include the timing and size of Series B capital availability, technology readiness relative to the peer set, and regulatory pathway clarity in climate-tech subsectors. The outlier peer (Commonwealth Fusion at $5.0B) underscores stage-dependent valuation volatility in this sector.
